## Real Material - Architect Learning Build (Domain 7: Hybrid Connectivity)

This section is the study-first architecture guide for hybrid routing control, encrypted enterprise connectivity, remote-user access, and deterministic failover design.

### Phase 1 - Foundations

#### Module 1: Hybrid Mental Model
- Hybrid networking is a routing-governance problem as much as a connectivity problem.
- Dedicated links, encrypted tunnels, and remote-user access solve different requirements.
- Path design must include both steady-state and failure-state behavior.

- Hi, welcome to this section, hybrid network in AWS.
- Now, hybrid networking is one of the most important topic for your exam.
- And when we talk about the hybrid network in AWS, typically there are multiple AWS services which comes into the play.
- But now, let's focus on the hybrid side of the network, which means what if you want to connect your on-premise data center to AWS network?
- And in that we will have EC2 instance with only the private IP, which means in order to reach to this EC2 instance from say customer network, we can only use the VPN connectivity.
- So we will use this LibreSwan VPN to configure this site to site VPN connectivity.
- Finally, here we will allow the SSH from this network only because if we want to test connectivity from EC2 instance B to EC2 instance A, we need to first SSH into that.
- Then we will also allow ICMP from AWS network so that we can test both way connectivity.
- So in this exercise, we will first set both the VPCs, we will launch the EC2 instances and then we will set up the VPN connectivity.
- And similarly, let's launch another instance that is EC2B for testing the connectivity.
- Second you need to change these algorithms because these are not considered to be the strongest cipher suits that is being used by the ipsec VPN connectivity.
- That means end-to-end connectivity is now working from EC2 instance B via the VPN server to the EC2 instance A.
- Now, additionally, you can also do SSH because we have opened the security group there, but this proves the end-to-end connectivity.
- And for having this connectivity, we will set up AWS client VPN endpoint.
- And further, in order to secure this connectivity, we will have the security group in the target network.

#### Module 2: Routing and BGP Basics
- Static routing is simple but limited for dynamic environments.
- BGP is the policy engine behind scalable hybrid path selection.
- Attributes and advertisement scope determine whether failover actually works.

##### Transcript-Enriched Learning Notes
- Hello, and welcome to this lecture, Dynamic Routing using Border Gateway Protocol.
- So in the earlier lecture, we understood the difference between the static routing and the dynamic routing.
- And we understood that in case of static routing, you have to modify the route entries in your route table, and you have to add the new routes that you want to send traffic to manually.
- And we also understood that in dynamic routing, these routes are propagated automatically.
- It's a dynamic routing protocol using path-vector.
- So any network, you can call it as like AS and this AS is interchange the routing information between each other.
- So in case of BGP, they work on path-vector protocol, which means they exchange the best part to reach to the destination.
- I will exchange that information with my neighbor BGP, and that's where the routes gets exchanged.
- Now, there are two types of the BGP.
- One is iBGP, or you can call it internal BGP.
- So it is like routing within one autonomous system.
- And what we are interested in, in eBGP, which is routing between the different AS's because we are connecting multiple networks, right?
- And that's where the routing would happen between two different AS's.
- So that's called eBGP.
- Now, how is this routing decision is influenced?
- Can I influence the routing?
- So through BGP can influence that I want to take one route or the other.
- Now there are certain parameters or features, or you can say attributes using which you can influence this routing mechanism.

#### Module 3: Connectivity Classes
- Site-to-Site VPN provides encrypted path establishment quickly.
- Direct Connect provides deterministic private transport.
- Client VPN is the workforce-access pattern, not a substitute for site connectivity.

### Phase 2 - Core Services

#### Site-to-Site VPN and Transit Patterns
- VPN is strong for encrypted connectivity and as a backup to dedicated links.
- Tunnel health, NAT-T, DPD, and propagation settings are architecture details, not trivia.
- Transit-aware hybrid design should separate network domains intentionally.

#### Direct Connect Design
- Use DX for predictable latency, bandwidth, and private-path requirements.
- Public, private, and transit VIF choices map directly to architecture intent.
- Resilient DX design means redundancy, BFD awareness, and failure testing.

#### Client VPN and Remote Access
- Client VPN is for user-to-network access with route and authorization control.
- Split tunnel versus full tunnel is a security and operational decision.
- Remote access must align with broader identity and segmentation policy.

### Phase 3 - Advanced Patterns

#### Pattern 1: DX Primary with VPN Backup
- Pair deterministic private connectivity with encrypted failover.
- Validate route preference so failures shift traffic predictably.
- Keep backup paths real, not decorative.

#### Pattern 2: BGP Policy Discipline
- Treat advertisements as production control, not setup noise.
- Path selection attributes should be documented and intentional.
- Many hybrid outages are policy mistakes, not transport failures.

#### Pattern 3: Segmented Hybrid Edge
- Separate enterprise network domains where trust and blast radius differ.
- Use transit abstractions and attachment design to enforce that separation.
- Keep user access, site access, and service access conceptually distinct.

### Phase 4 - System Design (Production)

#### Reference Hybrid Architecture
- Redundant private transport with encrypted backup, BGP-governed path preference, and controlled remote-user entry.
- Explicit separation between site connectivity, workforce connectivity, and service transit.
- Failure detection and route-policy design treated as first-class architecture components.

#### Scaling Considerations
- Prefix overlap and route sprawl become major pain points in enterprise estates.
- Hybrid designs are brittle when ownership of advertisements is unclear.
- Remote-user growth adds operational complexity around routes and access authorization.

#### Cost Considerations
- Circuits, VPN processing, and support complexity all matter.
- The cost of poor failover design usually exceeds the cost of redundant paths.
- Simpler policy models are cheaper to operate under pressure.

### Cross-Cloud Concept Mapping (Hybrid Connectivity)

#### Underlying Concept: Private Enterprise Transport
- AWS: Direct Connect and VIF models
- Azure: ExpressRoute and peering models
- GCP: Cloud Interconnect variants
- Trade-off: dedicated transport exists everywhere, but routing and segmentation models differ.

#### Underlying Concept: Encrypted Hybrid Backup and Remote Access
- AWS: Site-to-Site VPN and Client VPN
- Azure: VPN Gateway and P2S/S2S patterns
- GCP: HA VPN and remote-user access patterns
- Trade-off: encryption is portable, but operational tooling and route behavior vary.

### Real-World Use Cases from Transcript Signals
- Build enterprise hybrid networks with DX as primary transport and VPN as backup.
- Use BGP-driven policy to control steady-state and failover traffic decisions.
- Provide workforce access with Client VPN while keeping site and service connectivity separate.

### Constraints and Exam-Relevant Traps Captured
- Hybrid failover depends on route policy, not just redundant links.
- Client VPN, Site-to-Site VPN, and Direct Connect solve different access problems.
- BGP details become architecture details the moment resilience matters.

- Services: (none explicit)

Line 62:

Line 65:

Line 66:

Line 8:

Line 12:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 13:

Line 15:

Line 16:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 17:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: So in the regular case, if your customer gateway is publicly accessible, then you must allow inbound and outbound traffic for this two protocols and the ports UDP with 500 ports and ESP 50 protocol.

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 22:

Line 23:

Line 24:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 25:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 26:

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 30:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You have to allow UDP traffic on port 4500.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And once you do that on AWS side, you don't have to do anything because during the initial handshake, AWS understands that it is talking to the Nat device.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And it all happens as a part of Ike negotiation.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Customer Gateway, Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: And then AWS will start wrapping up this ESP 50 inside the UDP packet, and it will send request over port 4500 and later that will handle the UDP packets and will send the packets to the customer gateway where you can terminate the VPN.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 34:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: So whenever customer gateway is behind the Nat, then you have to enable the Nat traversal and you have to open these ports.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 35:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in this case, as I said, you have to allow inbound and outbound traffic for both this UDP ports.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 36:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Okay.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 37:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So I hope it is clear.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 38:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now with that let's just summarize what we discussed.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 39:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So AWS automatically detects the Nat during key negotiation and switches to UDP for 500 instead of directly sending traffic using ESP 50 protocol.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 40:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: So for Nat traversal, no configuration is required on AWS side, but on the customer gateway side.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 41:

Line 42:

- Key Insights: So let's try to understand how the Route Propagation happens in case of VPN, and you know that there is two types of routings that VPN supports, the Static routing and the Dynamic routing, and let's understand how it works with respect to AWS site-to-site VPN.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Customer Gateway, Site-to-Site VPN, Virtual Private Gateway

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Customer Gateway, Site-to-Site VPN, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: Now, in case of Static VPN, what happens, like you have to update the route tables on both sides of the connection, and you have to say like, if the traffic is going to particular destination on the right side, then the batch, the traffic should go through Virtual Private Gateway and similarly, route table you need to maintain on the Customer Gateway side, that if the destination is AWS CIDR, then the traffic should go through the Customer Gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Customer Gateway, Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: Now, if network changes on any side of the VPN, for example, on Customer Gateway side, you extend that network to some other network.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: That means you have for the VPN connection to a third network.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: In that case, AWS network won't know that now your Customer Gateway can also be used to reach to a third network, right?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: In that case on AWS side, you need to update your route table for your subnets of course and tell that if the traffic is going for that network C, then also it should take the path through VPN gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So that means all these route table update has to be done manually, that's a Static routing.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control

Line 11:

Line 12:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 13:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, one thing to note, there is that AWS side of the route tables, that means the route table, which are attached to subnets, cannot have more than a hundred propagated routes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control, Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: BGP, Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: Now that's a limitation on AWS side, which means if on the customer side, if you are sending more than hundred routes through BGP, your connection might break, and we don't know how the exact behavior it would be, but the chances are your VPN connection can get terminated because AWS side of the network cannot tolerate or cannot accept more than hundred propagated routes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal; Routing-policy and path-selection signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: So in that case, what you are to make sure that these BGP connections or BGP routes, you are not publishing more than a hundred routes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: Now, how you can restrict your number of routes to a hundred, maybe, if you are sending too many small CIDR routes, for example, you are sending 10.10.0.024, 10.10.1.24, Now, you know that all these ranges are a part of maybe a larger CIDR that is 10.10.0016 So what you can do, you can consolidate all these CIDRs into probably one big or large CIDR, and then you publish that or the BGP instead of sending the small CIDRs.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP

Line 22:

Line 24:

Line 12:

Line 14:

- Services: Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: And there is a reason to that because virtual private gateway is actually a gateway device, it's not ENI which is provisioned by AWS, where, because ENI is a part of your VPC, it's a part of your subnet.

Line 15:

- Services: (none explicit)

Line 14:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 38:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control, Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 39:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 41:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Virtual Private Gateway

Line 42:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control

Line 43:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: But with dynamic routing, with BGP you have ways to influence the decision here on the virtual private gateway side, saying that, always use tunnel one, because maybe I am pushing the more specific routes through tunnel a one.

Line 44:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 45:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: What that means is, maybe this server falls into a subnet range of 192.168.0.0/24 and so for this BGP session you can publish the CIDR as 192.168.0.0/24 and for tunnel two, you might want to advertise 192.168.0.0/16.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 46:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: Now /24 is more specific than /16, so VGW will always prefer the tunnel one for the return traffic, right?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 47:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: As you know, you can also influence the routing decision in case of BGP using something called ASPATH, that means if tunnel one has shorter ASPATH, VGW will always prefer that particular path rather than tunnel two path if it is longer than the tunnel one is ASPATH, so you can also influence that decision.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 48:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: When ASPATHs are having the same length, then you can use third BGP parameter, that is MED, multi-exit discriminator.

Line 49:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: What that means is the lowest MED value would take the preference.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 50:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP, Customer Gateway, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: So from BGP end, from the customer gateway end you can advertise different MED values for both the tunnels and here, VGW of course will choose the tunnel which has lower MED values.

Line 51:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP, Customer Gateway, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: Now this is only possible in case of dynamic routing because you are having to BGP peers, one at VGW and other at CGW end.

Line 52:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP

Line 53:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: But in this case, if you have lower MED values, of course VGW will use tunnel one always as a preferred tunnel and then the traffic will go back to your data center and there will be no asymmetric routing.

Line 54:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now the best part is, if tunnel one goes down for some reason, you have another tunnel up all the time, so all the traffic now will be routed through tunnel two.

Line 55:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: So we are basically influencing the tunnel decision on AWS side of the network using this BGP parameters.

Line 56:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this is very interesting and very important to know for you, may not be directly asked in a exam, but maybe in your day to day work you can analyze why you have asymmetric routing and how to fix that.

Line 57:
- Concepts: Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 58:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: So just a summary in case of static routing, active-active tunnel mode, AWS randomly select the tunnel for the traffic originating from AWS and make sure that you configure or enable the asymmetric routing on your customer gateway side of the firewall.

Line 59:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control, Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: In case of static routing with active-passive tunnel mode, that means only one tunnel is up at the moment, you don't have to really worry because always that tunnel will be used for both sides of the communication.

Line 60:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 61:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control, Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now in case of dynamic routing, of course it will make sense to have active-active tunnel mode only because, active-passive, you are just not leveraging the high availability of the tunnels.

- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity

- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Customer Gateway, Site-to-Site VPN

Line 10:

Line 11:

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: As I said, VGW will send R-U-THERE messages continuously and your customer gateway should response to those messages.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway
- Key Insights: Now, if customer gateway for some reason is not responding to your DPD packets for three consecutive times.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: The VGW will terminate the tunnel that's what the DPD is.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, few more things you need to understand about the DPD is that.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: By default, when DPD occurs, your gateway would delete the security associations, which it creates during the IPSec handshake and during that process the alternate IPSec tunnel can be used.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: If you have configured both the tunnel in Active-Active mode.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, default DPD timeout is 30 seconds, but then you can set it higher than the 30 seconds.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 23:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: DPD uses UDP port 500 and in case of NAT traversal, port 4500 to send this DPD packages, so make sure that these ports are enabled.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: Now, when DPD occurs, that means when the DPD timeout occurs, there are three actions that you can set on AWS side of the VPN connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 25:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You can say that, when DPD occurs, maybe you clear the connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: That means in the IPSec session, stop the tunnel and clear the routes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: Or you can say restart the IPSec IKE phase one, or take no action.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 28:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: So this is up to you, how you configure it, but the default action is to clear everything, that means you end your IPSec tunnel.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Customer, which uses the Dynamic Routing must support the DPD at their router.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 30:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: That means if your router does not support DPD and you are using Dynamic Routing using BGP, then that router won't be able to make the connection with AWS or VGW.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But for inactivity of your tunnel, in the sense that if you're not sending any data across your tunnel, even though the tunnels are up, everything is working, but there is no server on either side which is continuously or which is sending the data through that tunnel, now this is called idle connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Customer Gateway

Line 34:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity

- Key Insights: And if you set that timeout time, there is no packet flowing during that timeout period then also your VPN tunnel is terminated.

Line 35:

Line 36:

Line 37:

Line 38:

Line 39:

Line 42:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity, Resilience and Failure Detection

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

- Services: (none explicit)

Line 23:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 24:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But in this case, even if the hosts are sitting across the different networks, they can still be part of the same LAN and that is called virtual LAN (VLAN).

Line 25:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So if you see here, this host here on the left side is a part of the VLAN10 group and there are hosts on the other network as well.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Similarly for other hosts.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 28:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But still when the packet arrives at this router on the right side, it knows that the destination is this LAN and within that LAN there is that host.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 30:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 31:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 32:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 33:

Line 34:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 35:

Line 36:

Line 37:

Line 38:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 39:

Line 49:

Line 60:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 61:

Line 63:

Line 64:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 65:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 66:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 67:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 68:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

Line 69:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 70:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now with respect to the dedicated connection we just talked earlier, AWS will wait for maximum 90 days from the start of the billing.

Line 71:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 72:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: After 90 days, if you still have the connection there, it will start billing for the port hours.

Line 73:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 74:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

Line 81:

Line 82:

Line 86:

Line 90:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect

Line 91:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

- Key Insights: And this is typically done because you don't want probably one Gbps of Direct Connect connection.

Line 92:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

Line 93:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

- Services: (none explicit)

Line 97:

- Services: (none explicit)

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 99:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: Imagine a situation where you have centralized networking team who owns all Direct Connect connections and then different projects inside the organization need a connectivity to on-premise network and AWS.

Line 100:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: In that case, you could have your own dedicated connection or a hosted connection and this account is owned by the networking team, but then every project would have their own AWS account and the networking team can then create a VIF and say the owner of this VIF is project one team or project two team.

Line 101:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 102:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So as I said, when you create a VIF, you choose another AWS account who owns that VIF.

Line 103:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now with respect to the functionality and the connectivity, it does exactly same as with the public virtual interface or a private virtual interface.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 104:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: That means you can connect to AWS public resources like S3 or you can create a private VIF and can connect to AWS VPC.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 105:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And we already talked that the connection is owned by one account and the VIF is owned by another account.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 106:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: However, the VIF owner has to accept that VIF into his or her AWS account.

Line 107:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 108:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 109:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 110:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: And finally, it is used in the scenarios where, as I said, networking team manages the DX connection and provides the VIF as a service to the project teams.

Line 111:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So that essentially is the hosted VIF.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 112:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Okay, almost similar architecture just that AWS account here which is your central IT team account probably is the connection owner.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 113:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, this connection could be dedicated connection or a hosted connection, does not really matter for other business units, but what they can do is they can request the VIFs into their account, so the account owner here with a central IT team here will create a VIF into your account by providing your account ID, and then you would have to accept that VIF connection into your AWS account.

Line 114:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

Line 115:

Line 10:

Line 11:

Line 4:

Line 7:

Line 15:

Line 16:

- Services: (none explicit)

Line 19:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 20:

Line 9:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 10:

Line 11:

Line 14:

Line 6:

Line 7:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in the earlier lecture, if you remember, we already saw what PhD means in generic sense, and it helps you to take the failure in the network links and it detects that real quick so that you can fail over to the secondary link.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport, Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: And now in this lecture, let's understand how BFT is applied for the direct Connect connection and then how much failover time you would have if you enable the PhD and if you don't enable the BFT.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: So while using the multiple connection or using VPN backup, it's always important to have fast failure to a redundant connection.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control, Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: BFD, BGP
- Key Insights: So if you don't apply the BFD, so by default BGP waits for three keeps alive to fail at a hold down time of 90 seconds, so it takes up to 90 seconds to detect the failure if BFT is not applied.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Resilience and Failure Detection
- Services: BFD

Line 15:

- Key Insights: And if you talk about the calculation of one second typically sets the BFD liveliness detection interval to 300 milliseconds and then BFD liveliness detection count is three, which means if there are three consecutive failures, that means the peer does not send the response back for three consecutive packets in the interval of 300 milliseconds.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

### File: outputs/AWS/AWS Speciality/advanced-networking-specialty/14_Direct Connect (DX)/137_DX support for MTU and Jumbo Frames.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Hello, and welcome to this lecture.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now if you remember, our lecture about the network performance, in that lecture, we talked about the MTU and Jumbo Frames.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And if you remember, we said that Jumbo Frames allows the larger packet size to flow over the network, which ultimately improves the network performance and the throughput because less number of packets are required to transmit the data.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Services: Site-to-Site VPN
- Key Insights: And then we also saw when it comes to the VPC, what is an MTU that is supported when the traffic flows over to the internet or between VPCs over a VPC peering connection or through a VPN connection, right.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: Now, let's see how it is structured when it comes to the Direct Connect connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So just a quick recap, MTU is the size in bytes of the largest permissible packet that can be passed over the network.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: Now with respect to the DX connection, until 2018 DX did not support the Jumbo Frames.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: That means MTU was 1500.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: But post 2018, AWS started supporting the Jumbo Frames for DX connections.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: So as of today, you can obviously use Jumbo Frames on the Direct Connect connections, but for that, you have to enable that on the Direct Connect connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: Now, this does not mean that you can use Jumbo Frames, irrespective of the type of the virtual interfaces you are creating on top of Direct Connect connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And shortly, we'll see whether you can use Jumbo Frames with respect to the Private VIF or a Public VIF or a Transit VIF.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So very first thing to remember, public VIFs don't support Jumbo Frames, and when it comes to the Transit VIF or Private a VIF, Transit VIF support the MTU of 1500 or 8500.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So Transit VIF also supports, you can say Jumbo Frames, but the size is 8500.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: However, Private VIFs support the MTU of 9001 which is again, a Jumbo Frame.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect, Direct Connect Gateway, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: Now this is supported when Private VIF is attached to the Virtual Private gateway or Direct Connect gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: And this is required that when you have to use Jumbo Frames on the VIFs of course the underlying Direct Connect connection or LAG should also be supporting the Jumbo Frames.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Constraint or limitation signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So there is no point if the connection is not enabled for Jumbo Frames, then creating a VIFs with Jumbo Frames enabled doesn't help right?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this is how it looks like, you have the connection, which is Jumbo Frame enabled.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And on top of that connection, you can have Public VIF or a Private VIF or a Transit VIF.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Public VIF do not support Jumbo Frames, but Private VIF does and so as the Transit VIF.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So just remember this and this should be sufficient for your exam.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 23:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Just one more, last thing about the Jumbo Frames.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: Jumbo Frames are applied only to the propagated routes for the DX.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 25:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And if there is a static route, then the default 1500 MTU is used.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: And this is something again, I don't think so, really you need to remember all this, just remember that DX now support the Jumbo Frames and Public VIF do not support jumbo frames, where as Transit VIF and Private VIF support Jumbo Frames.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 27:

- Services: (none explicit)

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

Line 12:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 13:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 14:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 15:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 21:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 22:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context

- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport

- Key Insights: Now, if the connection is up, but still your VIFs are down, so you might've created a public VIF or a private VIF and those VIFs are not working, then you are to make sure the IP addresses that you provide while creating the VIFs, right, those are correct.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport, Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP

Line 25:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now those IPs must be owned by the customer, you know that, so you are to make sure it is correct, VLAN IDs are correct.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So what are tags you are using now?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You know that if it's the same connection VLAN tags should be unique across all VIFs on the same connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 28:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you have to make sure VLAN IDs are correct.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The router MAC address in the ARP table, that means...
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 30:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You know, ultimately at Layer 2, the IP addresses are mapped to the MAC addresses for the routers, right?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in that case, you are to make sure MAC addresses are correct.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: If not, then you can also try cleaning your ARP tables and also you are to make sure if it's a issue with the VLAN, then VLAN truncing is enabled at the intermediate devices.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now this is more on your side of the network because the connection from DirectConnect location to AWS is, again, managed by AWS, but from DirectConnect location to the on-premise data center, whatever network is there, if there are multiple hubs and routers in between you are to make sure VLAN truncing is enabled there as well.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 34:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And of course, if nothing out of this works, then contact AWS support.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 35:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: So now assuming that your connection is up, the VIFs are up, but still the traffic is not going, and you see that the BGP session is down.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 36:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: So for that, you are to make sure when you give the parameters for the BGP session, you give the correct ASN numbers.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 37:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, you know that if you are using the public VIF and you are doing a public ASN, that ASN has to be owned by the customer and AWS verifies that.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 38:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: Then you also provide the peer IPs for the BGP session.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 39:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Those needs to be correct as well.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 40:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, in case of public VIF you know that these IP addresses should also be owned by the customer.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 41:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Then comes the MD5 Authentication key.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 42:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Make sure that there are no additional spaces and you provide your own MD5 Auth key or you use the one generated by AWS.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 43:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But when you expose these configurations and apply the same key on your side of the router, that means customer side of the router, you don't make mistake while putting those keys.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 44:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Then you know that in case of private VIF you can't advertise, or you shouldn't advertise more than 100 prefixes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Constraint or limitation signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 45:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport, Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: Otherwise the BGP session goes down and for public VIF this limit is 1000 prefixes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal; Routing-policy and path-selection signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 46:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So no more than 1000 prefixes should be advertised from customer into AWS side.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 47:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: You also know that for BGP session, it uses TCP 179 port number.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 48:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you are make sure that the firewalls at customer end are not blocking this port.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 49:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: Otherwise you can always look at the BGP logs and again, if nothing works, your BGP session is still down, you can contact AWS support.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Routing-policy and path-selection signal.

Line 50:
- Concepts: Hybrid Routing Control
- Services: BGP
- Key Insights: Now let's take the final approach where everything is okay, connection is okay, VIFs are up, BGP session is also up, but still the traffic is not reaching to the destination.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Routing-policy and path-selection signal; Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 51:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in that case, it could be a problem with your maybe routing tables, but we can see that if you are advertising the right routes for the on-premises prefixes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 52:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Because on AWS end those routes will be propagated, and if your IP address is not part of the prefixes that you advertise, then AWS won't route that traffic to your network using the DirectConnect connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 53:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now for public VIF it should be publicly routable prefixes.

Line 54:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: We already talked about that.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 55:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now on AWS side, you are to make sure your security group, which is attached to EC2 machines or ENIs, are not blocking the traffic or at subnet level network access control lists are not blocking the traffic from the source IP addresses of your on-premise network.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 56:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And you should also check your VPC Route table that there is route exist to take that traffic from AWS to on-premise network.

Line 57:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now these routes could be static or these routes could be dynamic propagated one.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 58:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So make sure those routes are available, without that traffic cannot flow in either direction.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 31:

Line 55:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Transit Gateway

Line 56:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Transit Gateway
- Key Insights: We could have, but we are not, because Transit Gateway has a lot of charge when it comes to per GB data processing.

Line 57:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Typically this is $0.02 per GB.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 58:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Transit Gateway
- Key Insights: This looks small, but if this VPC is doing, you know, terabytes and petabytes of data transfer or this transit gateway, then this is a huge cost and it would make sense to avoid using transit gateway in such scenarios.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 59:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect, Virtual Private Gateway
- Key Insights: So the choice for us is why don't we directly use a private Vif you can have your private Vif over this direct connect location and then you can connect it to the gateway or you can directly connect private Vif to the virtual private gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 60:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But there is no cost for the gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 61:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So I would recommend that you use the gateway instead, because in the future, if there are many such vpcs to be connected, then you can connect through the same gateway over the same private wave.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 62:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So it would make sense that you directly connect that to this another gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 63:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Transit Gateway
- Key Insights: That way you avoid the cost of this transit gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 64:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Okay.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 65:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: Transit Gateway
- Key Insights: And finally, if you want to connect to the public services, like, for example, S3, again, you don't want to send that data through Transit Gateway or through your VPC because there would be additional cost.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 66:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Because if you want to send the data to S3 through VPC, you would want to have the VPC interface endpoint provisioned inside the VPC.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 67:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And that interface endpoint also has PER-GB data processing cost.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 68:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: AWS Direct Connect
- Key Insights: So it's better you directly use a public VIF there on the same direct connect connection.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 69:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And in that case you avoid all those charges.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 70:
- Concepts: Dedicated Network Transport
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now you know that for public Vif you need additional settings in the sense that you need public IP at both the ends and you have to make sure that you are using the firewalls at your router.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Dedicated transport and resiliency signal.

Line 71:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Because once you connect your network over a public wave, any AWS customer can send the data through your public wave to your public IPS.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 72:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you need to be just a little more careful and put the right firewall while using the public wave.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 73:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But definitely it saves you a lot of cost instead of using the transit wave or a private wave.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes hybrid networking operational detail.

Line 74:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this is how your architecture should look like if you are having your operation across multiple regions and data centers.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 75:
- Concepts: Hybrid Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: As I said, there could be multiple variants of this architecture, but I hope this gives you an overall picture of connectivity.

- Services: (none explicit)

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Encrypted hybrid-connectivity signal.

## Step 2 â€” Consolidation

### 1. Concepts List
- Dedicated Network Transport
- Hybrid Operational Context
- Hybrid Routing Control
- Hybrid Topology Patterns
- Private and Encrypted Connectivity
- Remote User Access
- Resilience and Failure Detection

### 2. Services List
- AWS Client VPN
- AWS Direct Connect
- AWS Global Accelerator
- BFD
- BGP
- Customer Gateway
- Direct Connect Gateway
- MACsec
- Site-to-Site VPN
- Transit Gateway
- Virtual Private Gateway

### 3. Features List
- accelerated
- bfd
- bgp
- cloudhub
- direct connect
- dpd
- ipsec
- macsec
- nat-t
- split tunnel
- vif
- vpn

### 4. Use Cases
- 092_Introduction to Hybrid networking.txt:3: And when we talk about the hybrid network in AWS, typically there are multiple AWS services which comes into the play.
- 092_Introduction to Hybrid networking.txt:8: And so far, we talked mostly about the VPC and then how to connect to other AWS services within AWS region through the VPC endpoints and through VPC pairing connection and transit gateways.
- 092_Introduction to Hybrid networking.txt:9: But now, let's focus on the hybrid side of the network, which means what if you want to connect your on-premise data center to AWS network?
- 092_Introduction to Hybrid networking.txt:10: And when we say AWS network, it means primarily AWS VPC or it could also mean connecting privately to other AWS services like S3 and/or any public IPs.
- 092_Introduction to Hybrid networking.txt:22: So for that, you have to connect from your machine to the client VPN endpoint, and then you get access to the AWS network.
- 093_Static Routing vs Dynamic Routing.txt:18: So when you work in AWS, there is also a default ASN, private ASN, which is assigned and which you can use to connect to other networks.
- 093_Static Routing vs Dynamic Routing.txt:29: Now when this configuration is done, your packets can flow from network A to network B.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:16: So in case of BGP, they work on path-vector protocol, which means they exchange the best part to reach to the destination.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:17: So if I know the parts of other AS's, how to reach them, what is the next stop?
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:26: Now what that means is, if I want to reach to a destination, what parameters I need to consider.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:28: Suppose there are multiple routes to reach to the same destination.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:34: But there is something called AS PATH, which is like how many hops are there to reach to the destination.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:36: So ASPATH would contain the number of the hops or which AS's I need to cross in order to reach to the destination.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:41: So if you have multiple exit points to reach to the target destination from your source network, then within your source network, which router you will choose to go to that destination, that is basically a local preference.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:80: They learn the route from the other AS, like if from AS 100, I want to reach to AS 400, then what route entry I should have.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:86: Now if I'm to reach to 400, of course, my next destination is 10, 40, zero, one, which is this router.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:93: And now if you can see, if I want to reach to AS 400, any IP address 400, it will take that path.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:95: Now, what if I want to reach to ASA 300 IP address?
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:100: So in this example, if you see my AS 400 knows how to reach to AS 300 using the hop 10, 30, zero, one, right?
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:114: Like AS 100 has now two entries to reach to AS 300.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:116: Suppose by default BGP chooses the, this path that is through AS 400 to reach to the AS 300 and somehow this link goes down, that means there is some problem with that network.
- 094_How Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) works_.txt:117: Now, BGP can't take other path because in routing table, it knows that there is one more path exists to reach to the target destination.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:5: So, as I said, ASPATH is like number of hops you have to go through in order to reach to the destination, right?
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:9: Now, additionally, if you could see, AS 400 is also connected to AS 200, and it has the routing information how to reach to AS 200.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:12: So now if you look at this, AS 100 has two routes to reach to the AS 200.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:22: That means if we talk about the bandwidth, we can get more bandwidth to reach from 100 to 200 if it goes via AS 400.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:61: So from AS 100, we have now 2 connections to reach two AS 400 through different destination routers in AS 400.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:63: Now, Multi Exit Discriminator will come into the picture, when local preference is the same, when ASPATH is the same, then only this is MED is looked at.
- 095_BGP Route selection - ASPATH, LOCAL_PREF, MED.txt:74: So there are multiple paths to reach to the same target AS, then you can have the MED values to influence the routing decision.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:14: So if you want to access any AWS resources, for example, your EC2 instances or your databases, then of course you just connect to AWS console, or you get a public IP of that AWS resource and connect it over the internet.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:19: Now, when it comes to accessing the AWS resources privately and securely, then there are different ways to access AWS resources.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:39: If you have your corporate data center and if you want to connect that privately to the AWS VPCs, then it will be AWS Site to Site VPN.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:64: So if you just double click into the VPN connection, you will see that there are two different tunnels and you can configure these tunnels into different mode, for example active active or active passive.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:68: And other way to connect to AWS is using the Transit gateway, right?
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:74: When we say host that means host which are there on premises and the host or EC2 instances or databases which are there on the AWS side.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:79: Further, when we say site to site VPN, it is layer three VPN.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:89: For example, gray that is generic routing encapsulation, and typically it supports multicast or broadcast.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:96: So for example, you can deploy those VPN solution on EC2 instances.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:104: Further, when you configure your VPN in the BGP mode, that is dynamic routing, you have to provide the autonomous system number that is ASN for your virtual private gateway.
- 096_Introduction to AWS Site-to-Site VPN.txt:110: Now just one more thing which is worth mentioning is that when you create the VPN connection and actually we will do an exercise to set that up.

- 099_Accelerated Site-to-Site VPN (Using AWS Global Accelerator).txt:7: So in general, when you create the site to site VPN connection, typically you will have your customer network somewhere at the distance place and then you will have your AWS region.
- 099_Accelerated Site-to-Site VPN (Using AWS Global Accelerator).txt:16: And that's where many a times you may face challenges, for example having increased latency or there is congestion in the traffic, or there is some network disruption somewhere in any of the network.
- 099_Accelerated Site-to-Site VPN (Using AWS Global Accelerator).txt:28: And that is because all these edge locations are connected to AWS region using AWS backbone network, which means when now traffic flows to the transit gateway over the VPN, first time it will hit the nearest edge location.
- 099_Accelerated Site-to-Site VPN (Using AWS Global Accelerator).txt:64: So whenever you create the VPN tunnel, you have to use these two IPS for setting up that VPN connection and not the one which by default Transit Gateway will provide otherwise.
- 099_Accelerated Site-to-Site VPN (Using AWS Global Accelerator).txt:79: Now because the pricing change I'm not quoting how the charge is, but just remember that it cost you little extra when you enable the acceleration on your VPN connection, right?
- 100_VPN NAT Traversal (NAT-T).txt:14: And 50 is a protocol ID, for example TCP protocol six.
- 100_VPN NAT Traversal (NAT-T).txt:19: And now let's look at the scenario when your customer gateway is behind the Nat device.
- 100_VPN NAT Traversal (NAT-T).txt:34: So whenever customer gateway is behind the Nat, then you have to enable the Nat traversal and you have to open these ports.
- 100_VPN NAT Traversal (NAT-T).txt:46: You know that when you enable the accelerated VPN using AWS Global Accelerator, you have to enable the Nat traversal on the customer gateway device.
- 101_VPN Route Propagation (Static vs Dynamic).txt:5: Now, if network changes on any side of the VPN, for example, on Customer Gateway side, you extend that network to some other network.

- 102_VPN Transitive Routing scenarios.txt:3: Now, if your corporate data center is connected to AWS VPC using site-to-site VPN connection via virtual private gateway, then if you're sending traffic from your on-premise network to AWS, now let's see what happens, suppose if you want to reach to a peered VPC through that virtual private gateway connection, or you want to reach to say internet via internet gateway on AWS side of the network.
- 102_VPN Transitive Routing scenarios.txt:12: And what we mean that on corporate data center, you might not have outbound internet connection, but you're trying to reach to the internet by going through the VPN tunnel, and then from the IGW, is that possible?

- 102_VPN Transitive Routing scenarios.txt:56: Now, what if I want to reach to internet using on-premise NAT devices?
- 102_VPN Transitive Routing scenarios.txt:60: That means when a traffic originates from VPC, all the routing rules which we configured here would apply.

- 103_VPN Tunnels - Active_Passive Mode.txt:9: Now there is one problem, when VGW receives this traffic from AWS EC2 instance, it has choice to send this traffic back through tunnel one and tunnel two.
- 103_VPN Tunnels - Active_Passive Mode.txt:18: Now, if your traffic originates on AWS side of the network, for example, this EC2 instance here wants to send some traffic to any of those servers in corporate data center, it will send the packet to virtual private gateway and then this VGW will randomly select one of the tunnel, either tunnel 1 or tunnel two, you can't really influence that decision in case of static routing.
- 103_VPN Tunnels - Active_Passive Mode.txt:23: So tunnel two is for example down, if you remember our hands-on exercise, we had set up opens one tunnel and we had configured only one tunnel at that time, we did not configure another tunnel.

- 104_VPN Dead Peer Detection (DPD).txt:20: By default, when DPD occurs, your gateway would delete the security associations, which it creates during the IPSec handshake and during that process the alternate IPSec tunnel can be used.
- 104_VPN Dead Peer Detection (DPD).txt:24: Now, when DPD occurs, that means when the DPD timeout occurs, there are three actions that you can set on AWS side of the VPN connection.
- 104_VPN Dead Peer Detection (DPD).txt:25: You can say that, when DPD occurs, maybe you clear the connection.
- 104_VPN Dead Peer Detection (DPD).txt:37: And when this timeout occurs, in that case, you have to re-establish the VPN connection.

- 114_Direct Connect network requirements.txt:27: Now, how is it possible because when the traffic flows from host A here to host B on the right side, the traffic goes through the same network.

- 114_Direct Connect network requirements.txt:37: And when you send multiple VLANs through the same point to point network, it is called trunk.

- 116_Direct Connect Connection Types - Dedicated vs Hosted.txt:13: Now, what does that mean is you can get the bandwidth in multiple of say 50, 100, 200, 300 or 500 Mbps, or in some cases when 1 Gbps, Gbps 5 and 10 Gbps as well.

- 116_Direct Connect Connection Types - Dedicated vs Hosted.txt:39: Now, when you request a Hosted Connection, you would request it to the partner.

- 122_Public VIF.txt:44: So one thing to understand is that or to remember is that from customer router (indistinct) when you publish or advertise your BGP routes, maximum you can advertise 1000 routes and that's a hard limit, which means that if you send or advertise more than 1000 routes from your end, then it is possible that AWS route table might not be able to cope up with that and there is some problem with the BGP session.
- 122_Public VIF.txt:49: So for example, if there are two /28 prefixes with the same base CIDR, then maybe you can combine two /27 prefixes.
